sections.content:
Decision records in this package use their `tags` field as topic labels for browsing decisions by topic (user story S3: browse by date and topic).

Each tag is a short noun phrase identifying the topic area of the decision (e.g. `finance`, `space`, `governance`, `artists`). Tags are free-form strings; no closed vocabulary is enforced in this package version. Future packages may install a Vocabulary (RFC-006) to curate the tag set.

Multi-topic decisions may carry multiple tags. When filtering by topic in a UI or via CLI, match on `tags` containing the chosen topic key.
sections.name: convention

sections.content:
A decision about exhibition insurance -> tags: ["artists", "finance"]
A decision about wall mounting standards -> tags: ["space", "installation"]
A governance decision about meeting quorum -> tags: ["governance"]
sections.name: examples

sections.content: The decision_log DocumentView lifecycle exclusion (exclude superseded/abandoned by default, show-all config) is deferred until the RFC-M schema update (srs#82) lands and srs-rust#180 (render filter) is complete. Once those ship, the decision-log section source should be updated with excludeLifecycleStates: ["superseded", "abandoned"].
sections.name: gap
